page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core and no hyperthreading. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB per core. For local storage, it includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score and Geekbench multi-core tests, alongside strong memory bandwidth read and write speeds. Database operations and Redis SET operations also registered strong performance, with Redis reaching 101.36 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average category, and text generation for small language models is relatively poor.

The r7gd.16xlarge offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads due to its bundled local NVMe SSDs and custom ARM-based Graviton silicon. The dedicated core allocation and high memory bandwidth make it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving. It is less optimal for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based server powered by 64 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, offering 8.0 GB per core, and includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core and memory performance, with strong results in database operations and Redis SET throughput, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy applications. The r7gd.16xlarge is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, built for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-capacity local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding databases.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core benchmark performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and software compilation
